shared_properties: Base prisoner proportions, tunic layout, and 16x24px collision footprint stay aligned with the fall loop.
Primary cloth color remains #D6D0C4 beneath the damage flash treatment.
Outline weight stays at 1px so the hurt state remains readable against dark dungeon backgrounds.
differences: Sprite sheet is 32x24px with two frames tuned for a fast 12 FPS damage pulse.
Accent red flash pixels and a brief torso recoil replace the neutral descent posture.
Face and shoulder clusters tighten inward to signal impact during the 1.5 second invulnerability window.

context.usage: Damage feedback pose used after hazard contact and during invulnerability flicker.

shape.silhouette: Recoiling prisoner with compressed torso and sharper shoulder angle.
shape.collision_read: The hurt flash stays within the stable 16x24px footprint for invulnerability readability.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet size is exactly 32x24px with two 16x24px frames.
Red flash coverage stays between 20% and 35% of the painted pixels so the damage cue reads immediately.
The hurt loop feels sharper than the fall loop at 12 FPS while keeping the player silhouette recognizable.

reference_images.label: slip_down_player_reference
reference_images.purpose: Grounds prisoner proportions, cloth folds, and animation posing for the active sprite.

reference_images.label: slip_down_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds dungeon lighting, palette temperature, and surrounding tile contrast for the active sprite.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteSheetBundle with a TextureAtlasLayout and short invulnerability flash timer.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load through AssetServer during the Loading state and bind it as the damage-state atlas in the player resource.
runtime_notes: Use Bevy 0.13 with ImagePlugin::default_nearest() so every asset stays pixel-perfect at 4x upscale.
Drive gameplay motion in FixedUpdate at 60 FPS and keep art timing on deterministic timers.
Combine atlas swaps with a visibility flicker timer instead of recoloring the sprite at runtime.
